title: Where Did I Learn That Wanting Was Wrong
subtitle: A journal practice for uncovering and releasing the source of financial guilt
excerpt: A set of journal prompts for Day 15 of the money arc. The writing traces where the guilt about wanting wealth came from, who taught it, and what it has cost. It ends with a permission statement the writer crafts for themselves.
sourceNotes: Prompt material from Day 15 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448). Additional prompts original to homemade.education.

practice.whenToUse: On a morning in Week 3 of the money arc, when the work is moving into guilt and scarcity thinking — and you want to trace those beliefs to their source.

content.text: Journal prompts for Day 15 of the money arc. Use them in the morning, sitting up. The writing moves from where financial guilt came from, to what it has cost, to a permission statement you write for yourself.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
journal prompts as practice
first.
The practice
Warm-up
Where do you still feel guilty for wanting more money, freedom, or luxury?
Write freely. Don't edit or justify. Get the guilt on the page as it actually is.
Who taught you that wanting wealth was wrong or selfish?
Name the source — a parent, a teacher, a message. You don't need to judge them. Just name where it came from.
Closer in
What has that guilt cost you?
What have you not asked for, not allowed yourself to want, or not received because wanting felt wrong? Write the specific things, not general statements.
What blessings in your life remind you that receiving more allows you to give and share more?
Write what becomes possible for others when you have more. Concrete examples — people, things, situations.
Write a permission statement for your own wanting.
Start with 'I give myself permission to want...' and finish it in your own words. Write it twice. Say it aloud.
Where this practice comes from
Built from the Day 15 prompts of
The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow
(Rebecca J Page, 2025). Additional prompts original to homemade.education.
